title: Bridge Essay: Physiological feelings.
concept: Interoception — visceral perception, cardiac interoception, insula, vagal signaling, felt aliveness.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Body-energy** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Interoception — visceral perception, cardiac interoception, insula, vagal signaling, felt aliveness.

**Physiological feelings.** - Neurosci Biobehav Rev (2019). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2019.05.002. PMID: 31125635. The role of peripheral physiology in the experience of emotion has been debated since the 19th century following the seminal proposal by William James that somatic responses to stimuli determine subjective emotion. Subsequent views have integrated the forebrain's ability to initiate, represent and simulate such physiological events. Modern affective neuroscience envisions an interacting network of "bottom-up" and "top-down" signaling in which the peripheral (PNS) and central nervous systems both
